topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The delivery notification pinged on my phone, a small, almost irritating sound in the otherwise silent apartment. I picked up the package, the cardboard cool against my palm. It was addressed to the previous resident, a faded script that felt like an unnecessary formality. A faint smirk played on my lips as I surveyed the plain brown box. I didn't need to open it; I already knew what it contained—probably old junk. I tossed it onto the entryway table, the sound barely registering. I was in control of this space, and nothing, not even old mail, could rattle my composure. I straightened my jacket, the crisp fabric feeling like armor.

[story 2]

When the courier handed over the parcel, I met his gaze without flinching. "For the last tenant?" I asked, my voice level, carrying a distinct edge of challenge. He blinked, clearly taken aback by the directness. I took the box, feeling the weight of it assert itself in my grip. It was just a package, nothing more. I carried it directly to the recycling bin, bypassing the usual sorting process. There was no need to investigate; my perception of the situation was already complete. I watched the contents disappear into the refuse, a small, satisfying act of dismissal.

[story 3]

The doorbell rang, and I opened the door to the delivery person. I took the package with a brisk nod, my posture radiating an unshakeable assurance. "This is for the former occupant," I stated, my tone suggesting this was a minor administrative detail, nothing requiring deep thought. I didn't linger. I stepped back into my apartment, placing the item on the counter with deliberate precision. My movements were economical and sharp. I glanced at the door, already planning my next move, entirely unbothered by the anonymous item left behind.
